attributes.description: Alerts when a HAProxy backend has zero active servers (`haproxy.active < 1` on service_name=BACKEND). The backend cannot serve any traffic.
attributes.name: [HAProxy OTel] Backend has no active servers

investigation_guide.blob:
## HAProxy backend has no active servers

### What fired
`haproxy.active` on a BACKEND row dropped to 0 during the window.

### Why it matters
A backend with no active servers rejects all requests routed to it. This is a complete outage for any frontend using that backend.

### Triage
1. Drill into Server dashboard to see individual server health checks and downtime.
2. Check `haproxy.failed_checks` increments and backup server availability.
3. Look at server-side causes (crashes, networking).

### Remediation
- Restore at least one healthy server in the backend.
- Route traffic to a backup backend if configured.

### Tuning
- Always P1 — do not loosen.
